findings.criterion: Scenario aligns with the drag-and-drop add-to-cart behaviour: preconditions, drag/drop action targeting the drop-area, and user-observable cart outcomes (title, price, quantity including repeated additions).

evidence.quote: The system should allow users to drag and drop product items(data-testid="product-item-1", data-testid="product-item-2", etc.) into a designated drop area (id="div1", data-testid="drop-area") to add them to the cart.
When the user drags the product item with data-testid "product-item-3" and drops it into the drop area with data-testid "drop-area"
Then the cart display should show a product with title "Mastering JavaScript"

findings.reasoning: The scenario provides the required preconditions ('the webpage is loaded' and the product is visible), performs the explicit drag-and-drop action into the declared drop target ('data-testid "drop-area"'), and asserts the user-observable outcomes: product title, price, and an incremented quantity of "2" after two drops. The use of product-item-3 is consistent with the requirement's pattern of product-item-N identifiers. The scenario therefore covers the behaviour contract's core action and expected observable effects.
